name: Stripe
one_liner: Stripe is a developer-focused payment processing platform that enables businesses to accept payments and manage financial transactions through its APIs.

evidence.claim: The Stripe API is organized around REST with predictable resource-oriented URLs and returns JSON-encoded responses.
Stripe's API spans hundreds of endpoints across various products including Payments, Billing, Connect, and Tax.
Search citation: Stripe Integration: Setting Up & Understanding Stripe's API - Free Course Preview | Part 1
Search citation: How API changes flow into Stripe's developer products | Stripe Dot Dev Blog

api_notes: The API is organized around REST, uses JSON-encoded responses, and offers both v1 and v2 namespaces. It provides extensive SDKs and is supported by OpenAPI specifications.
mcp_notes: There is no official or well-known community MCP server provided by Stripe. Stripe focuses on OpenAPI specifications and SDKs for its developer integrations.
auth_notes: Stripe uses API keys (sent as Bearer tokens in the Authorization header) to authenticate requests.
access_notes: Developers can sign up for a Stripe account and access API credentials in test mode immediately via a self-serve process.
